sourceMovie.title: The Empire of Corpses
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Empire of Corpses (2015) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Degeneration
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Empire of Corpses clicked for you, Resident Evil: Degeneration (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Vampire Hunter D: Bloodlust
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Empire of Corpses clicked for you, Vampire Hunter D: Bloodlust (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: GANTZ:O
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Empire of Corpses clicked for you, GANTZ:O (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and science fiction, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Empire of Corpses?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil: Degeneration, Vampire Hunter D: Bloodlust, GANTZ:O—each shares DNA with The Empire of Corpses while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Empire of Corpses?
faq.answer: The Empire of Corpses (2015) is best described as Animation, Horror, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Empire of Corpses part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Project Itoh Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
